skill.name: git-guardrails-claude-code
skill.description: Set up Claude Code hooks to block dangerous git commands (push, reset --hard, clean, branch -D, etc.) before they execute. Use when user wants to prevent destructive git operations, add git safety hooks, or block git push/reset in Claude Code.
skill.summary: Install PreToolUse hooks that prevent Claude from running destructive git commands like force push and hard reset.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: This is a defensive security skill that sets up Claude Code hooks to block dangerous git commands. The static analyzer flagged several patterns that are false positives: 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are misidentified JSON template syntax in markdown documentation; 'Hidden file in home directory' findings reference the legitimate Claude Code config path ~/.claude/settings.json; and 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' was triggered by the word 'hard' in git reset --hard patterns. The shell script is a straightforward pattern-matching guardrail with no malicious intent. It only blocks commands and never executes any untrusted input.

medium_findings.title: Shell script with stdin input parsing
medium_findings.description: The block-dangerous-git.sh script reads from stdin and uses jq to parse tool input. While this is a legitimate hook pattern, the script does not validate or sanitize the input before pattern matching. This is by design for a guardrail hook, but the grep patterns are not anchored and could theoretically match unintended commands. Low practical risk since the script only reads and matches, never executes.

low_findings.title: References to hidden config directories
low_findings.description: Documentation references ~/.claude/settings.json for global Claude Code configuration. This is the standard Claude Code config path, not a suspicious location. False positive from the static analyzer.

content.user_title: Block Dangerous Git Commands in Claude Code
content.value_statement: Claude can accidentally run destructive git operations like force push or hard reset. This skill installs hooks that block these commands before they execute, protecting your work from accidental data loss.
actual_capabilities: Installs a PreToolUse hook in Claude Code settings to intercept Bash tool calls
Blocks git push in all variants including --force and --force-with-lease
Blocks git reset --hard to prevent losing uncommitted changes
Blocks git clean -f and git clean -fd to prevent deleting untracked files
Blocks git branch -D (force delete) to prevent losing branch work
Supports both project-level (.claude/settings.json) and global (~/.claude/settings.json) installation
limitations: Only blocks commands matching exact substring patterns; custom dangerous commands must be added manually
Does not block git operations that bypass the Bash tool (e.g., direct terminal use by the user)
Requires jq to be installed on the system for input parsing
The grep patterns are not anchored, so variations like extra whitespace could affect matching

use_cases.title: Prevent accidental force push in team projects
use_cases.description: A team lead wants to ensure Claude never accidentally force pushes to shared branches. This skill installs a hook that blocks all push attempts.

use_cases.title: Protect uncommitted work during AI-assisted coding
use_cases.description: A solo developer uses Claude Code for rapid prototyping and wants to prevent accidental hard resets that would destroy experimental changes.

use_cases.title: Enforce safe git practices in CI environments
use_cases.description: DevOps engineers setting up Claude Code in CI/CD pipelines need to prevent destructive git operations in automated environments.

prompt_templates.title: Basic installation
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up git guardrails for this project so Claude cannot run dangerous git commands.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time setup for a new project

prompt_templates.title: Global installation
prompt_templates.prompt: Install git guardrails globally so they apply to all my Claude Code projects.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants protection across all projects

prompt_templates.title: Customize blocked patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Add 'git stash drop' to the blocked commands list and update the hook script.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to block additional destructive commands

prompt_templates.title: Verify the hook works
prompt_templates.prompt: Test that the guardrail hook is working by simulating a blocked command.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to confirm the installation is functional

output_examples.input: I want to push my changes to origin
output: I'll set up a git guardrail hook to prevent accidental pushes.
First, do you want this for just this project or globally?
For this project, I'll create .claude/hooks/block-dangerous-git.sh and add a PreToolUse hook to .claude/settings.json.
The hook will block: git push, git reset --hard, git clean -f/fd, git branch -D, git checkout ., and git restore .

output_examples.input: Try running: git push origin main
output: BLOCKED: 'git push origin main' matches dangerous pattern 'git push'. The user has prevented you from doing this.

best_practices: Always ask the user whether they want project-level or global installation before proceeding
Merge with existing settings.json rather than overwriting to preserve other configurations
Make the hook script executable with chmod +x after copying
Test the hook after installation with the echo | script command from the documentation
anti_patterns: Do not install globally without the user explicitly choosing that option
Do not overwrite existing settings.json files; always merge the hook configuration
Do not remove existing safety checks like the dangerous command patterns unless the user requests it

faq.question: What git commands does this skill block?
faq.answer: It blocks git push (all variants), git reset --hard, git clean -f and -fd, git branch -D, git checkout ., and git restore ..

faq.question: Can I add my own commands to the block list?
faq.answer: Yes. After installation, edit the hook script and add new patterns to the DANGEROUS_PATTERNS array.

faq.question: Does this affect the user running git directly in a terminal?
faq.answer: No. The hook only intercepts commands that Claude Code tries to run through the Bash tool. Commands you run yourself are not affected.

faq.question: What happens when a command is blocked?
faq.answer: The script exits with code 2 and prints a BLOCKED message to stderr. Claude sees this message and knows it cannot perform that action.

faq.question: Do I need jq installed?
faq.answer: Yes. The hook script uses jq to parse the tool input JSON. Most systems have it, but you may need to install it manually.

faq.question: Can I install this for just one project?
faq.answer: Yes. Choose project-level installation to add the hook to .claude/settings.json in the current repository only.
